pattern: The best candidate is always one of the whole strings, so test each string and keep the longest that is not a subsequence of any other string in the list.
hints: Any uncommon subsequence can be extended to a whole string, so only the full strings need checking.
A string qualifies when it is not a subsequence of any other string in the array (duplicates of itself disqualify it).
Among all qualifying strings return the longest length, or -1 if none qualify.

edge_inputs.description: three distinct equal-length strings each uncommon
shorter string is a subsequence of a duplicated longer one
all strings identical so none qualify
longest is a subsequence of another so a shorter one wins
two strings, neither a subsequence of the other
duplicate of the longest disqualifies it entirely

stress.description: many short strings from a tiny alphabet to force subsequence overlaps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 40)
    strs = []
    for _ in range(n):
        L = rng.randint(1, 6)
        strs.append(''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(L)))
    return [strs]
